list.name: DB event(blue line)
list.rawQuery:
SELECT time, string_agg(event_content, E'\n\n') AS text, 'PolarDB,' || string_agg(distinct event_type, ',') AS tags FROM ( SELECT time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time, event_type AS event_type, E'Type: ' || event_type || E', Total Event Counts: ' || COUNT(*) || '
Sample:
' || MAX(event_content) || E'
' AS event_content, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_event WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND level != 'INFO' AND (event_type NOT IN ('ERROR', 'FATAL') OR (event_type IN ('ERROR', 'FATAL') AND split_part(event_content, E'\t', 4) IN ('', 'replicator', 'postgres', 'aurora') AND event_content NOT LIKE '%polar_is_available%' AND event_content NOT LIKE '%polar_parameter_manager%')) GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), event_type ) a GROUP BY time ORDER BY 1

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Database compute nodes are categorized as read-write (RW) nodes or read-only (RO) nodes.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % ((($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)::integer) AS time,
 MAX(role) AS Role
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.description:

targets.rawSql:

 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 (AVG(cpu_user_usage) + AVG(pfsd_cpu_user / cpu_cores) + AVG(cpu_sys_usage) + AVG(pfsd_cpu_sys / cpu_cores))

WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Disk Space Usage Rate

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Average TPS during the current time interval.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- The metric is obtained from the pg_stat_database view as `SUM(xact_commit) + SUM(xact_rollback)`, and a delta is calculated during collection.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(commits_delta+rollbacks_delta) AS TPS
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: TPS

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Current active connections, indicating the database workload during the time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Source: pg_stat_activity view. The metric is calculated as `SUM(CASE WHEN state IN ('active') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)` to count active backend sessions.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(active_connections) AS active_connections
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- The average buffer hit rate of database block reads within the time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_database view. blks_hit: Number of blocks hit in the buffer cache (no disk read required). blks_read: Number of blocks read from disk. Hit ratio: blks_hit / (blks_hit + blks_read).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(blks_hit_delta / (blks_hit_delta + blks_read_delta + 1))
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Current delay of the slowest RO instance attached to the RW instance.
- This panel is displayed only for RW nodes.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Metric source: replay_lag in the pg_stat_replication view. The maximum value is selected for entries where application_name matches replica%, indicating the current maximum delay of RO nodes.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 MAX(replay_lag_in_ms) AS replay
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_pg_stat_replication, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND application_name like 'replica%'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: RO Synchronization Delay

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- This histogram shows the average number of sessions per second waiting on each event. If a session is not waiting, it is considered to be using CPU. The concept aligns with Oracle ASH.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Wait events are collected from the pg_stat_activity view at a one-second interval, using the fields wait_event_type and wait_event. A session with a NULL value in wait_event is considered to be using CPU.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 wait_event_type::text || ':' || wait_event AS metric,
 CASE WHEN ($__interval_ms / 1000) >= 3600 THEN ROUND(CAST(SUM(wait_count) AS NUMERIC) / (($__interval_ms / 3600 / 1000)::INTEGER), 2)::FLOAT
 WHEN ($__interval_ms / 1000) >= 60 THEN ROUND(CAST(SUM(wait_count) AS NUMERIC) / (($__interval_ms / 60 / 1000)::INTEGER), 2)::FLOAT
 ELSE ROUND(CAST(SUM(wait_count) AS NUMERIC) / (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), 2)::FLOAT END AS wait_count
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_aas_history, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), metric
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Average Active Session

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Database User Mode: CPU usage in user mode
- Database System Mode: CPU usage in system mode

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(cpu_user_usage) + AVG(pfsd_cpu_user / cpu_cores) AS database_user_mode,
 AVG(cpu_sys_usage) + AVG(pfsd_cpu_sys / cpu_cores) AS database_system_mode
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: CPU Usage (%)

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Stacked view of CPU usage by process type over the selected time window, including both user and system mode.
- Since client backend processes represent database sessions that can fluctuate over time, precise measurement of incremental CPU usage is challenging. This may lead to some *estimation error* in the reported values.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- CPU usage is collected via the polar_stat_process view by reading /proc/<pid>/stat. The values are cumulative and have been differenced over the specified time window for display.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT time, metric, cpu FROM (
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 backend_type AS metric,
 AVG(cpu_user+cpu_sys) / (SELECT cpu_cores FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) ORDER BY time DESC LIMIT 1) AS cpu
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_process, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND
 $__unixEpochFilter(time)
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), backend_type
UNION ALL
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 'postmaster' AS metric,
 AVG(postmaster_cpu) AS cpu
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND
 $__unixEpochFilter(time)
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
UNION ALL
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 'logger' AS metric,
 AVG(logger_cpu) AS cpu
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND
 $__unixEpochFilter(time)
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
UNION ALL
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 'pgstat' AS metric,
 AVG(pgstat_cpu) AS cpu
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND
 $__unixEpochFilter(time)
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
) a ORDER BY time

panels.title: CPU Usage by Process Type (stacked)

panels.title: Memory Usage (%)

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Summary of private memory usage by process type within the specified time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the rss column in the polar_stat_process view, obtain the Rss and Share values by reading the /proc/%pid/statm file corresponding to each process, and finally output the calculated `Rss - Share` as the process's private memory consumption.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT time, metric, rss FROM (
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 backend_type AS metric,
 AVG(rss) AS rss
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_process, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), backend_type
UNION ALL
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 'postmaster' AS metric,
 AVG(postmaster_mem) AS rss
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
UNION ALL
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 'pgstat' AS metric,
 AVG(pgstat_mem) AS rss
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
UNION ALL
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 'logger' AS metric,
 AVG(logger_mem) AS rss
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
) a ORDER BY time

panels.title: Memory: Stacked by Process Type

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Top processes by private memory usage within the specified time window, including the collection timestamp, RSS values, and query information.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- polar_stat_process view, filtered by RSS usage.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT pid AS pid, rss_mb AS "RSS(MB)", TO_CHAR(TO_TIMESTAMP(time), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS timepoint, query AS query
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_max_memory_sql 
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 ORDER BY rss_mb DESC, time DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Memory-Consuming Sessions (Over 200MB)

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- List the Memory Context information of the process that consumes the most memory within the current time window, to gain a more granular view of its memory usage.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- The process with the highest RSS is identified from the polar_stat_process view. Its memory contexts are then fetched using `polar_get_mcxt()`, and the top few largest memory contexts are returned as the final result.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT pid AS "pid", name AS "Memory Context", totalspace / 1024 / 1024 AS "MB", TO_CHAR(TO_TIMESTAMP(time), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS "timepoint"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_max_memory_backend_memory_context
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 ORDER BY totalspace DESC, time DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Average Latency

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the IOPS distribution of read operations across different file types on the shared storage medium within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Aggregated I/O statistics from `polar_stat_io_info()` grouped by process type.
- Detailed I/O information for each process is displayed using the polar_stat_io_info function, showing which types of files (e.g., WAL, data, log, index) the process accessed and what operations (e.g., seek, read, write, fsync, falloc) were performed.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 filetype AS metric,
 AVG(read_count) AS read_iops
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_io_info, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND fileloc='pfs'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), filetype
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: PFS open iops

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the I/O bandwidth distribution of read operations across different file types on the shared storage medium within the current time window.


Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Aggregating I/O information across each process type dimension via the polar_stat_io_info function.
- I/O activity per process is visualized using polar_stat_io_info including file type (WAL, data, log, index) and operation type (read, write).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,

 AVG(read_throughput) AS read_throughput
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_io_info, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)

panels.title: PFS write bps

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the read operation latency within the current time window, including both the shared storage medium and local directories.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG((num_lessthan200us+0.00001) / (num_lessthan200us+num_lessthan400us+num_lessthan1ms+num_lessthan10ms+num_lessthan100ms+num_morethan100ms+0.00001)) AS "[0, 200us]",
 AVG(num_lessthan400us / (num_lessthan200us+num_lessthan400us+num_lessthan1ms+num_lessthan10ms+num_lessthan100ms+num_morethan100ms+0.00001)) AS "(200us, 400us]",

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the total, active, and idle connection counts of the database instance within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_activity view.
- Active connections count entries with `state = 'active'`, idle connections count entries with `state = 'idle'`, and total connections equal the total rows in pg_stat_activity.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(connections) AS total,
 AVG(active_connections) AS active,
 AVG(idle_connections) AS idle
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Connection

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the active and idle transaction counts of the database instance within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_activity view
- Number of active transactions refers to entries with `state = 'active'`, and number of idle transactions refers to entries with state in `['idle in transaction', 'idle in transaction (aborted)']`.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(active_transactions) AS active,
 AVG(idle_transactions) AS idle
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Transaction

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the total number of commits, total number of rollbacks, and TPS within the current time window. TPS is the sum of commit and rollback counts. These metrics reflect the operational status of the database instance.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Calculate based on the total commit and rollback counts from the pg_stat_database view.
- Commit count: xact_commit; rollback count: xact_rollback; TPS is the sum of the two.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(commits_delta) AS commits,
 AVG(rollbacks_delta) AS rollbacks,
 AVG(commits_delta+rollbacks_delta) AS TPS
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: TPS

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the read, update, and delete operations on tuples within the current time window for the database instance. These metrics reflect the operational status and help assess the performance of the database.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Calculate the tuple-related metrics by using the pg_stat_database view. Since the corresponding fields in this view are cumulative values, the difference per second is computed during collection.
- The corresponding fields in the pg_stat_database view are as follows, all of which are cumulative values: tup_returned: Number of tuples (rows) returned by queries since the database was started. tup_fetched: Number of tuples physically fetched from disk by queries since the database was started. tup_inserted: Number of tuples inserted since the database was started. tup_updated: Number of tuples updated since the database was started. tup_deleted: Number of tuples deleted since the database was started.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(tup_returned_delta) AS tup_returned,
 AVG(tup_fetched_delta) AS tup_fetched,
 AVG(tup_inserted_delta) AS tup_inserted,
 AVG(tup_updated_delta) AS tup_updated,
 AVG(tup_deleted_delta) AS tup_deleted
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Rows

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- This reflects the current age of the database, which is related to the oldest frozen transaction ID. A high database age typically indicates long-running transactions or infrequent VACUUM operations. When the database age becomes very large (e.g., over 1.2 billion), it can impact performance and increase the risk of transaction ID wraparound (which occurs at over 2.1 billion).

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Calculate the `AGE(datfrozenxid)` field for each database from the pg_database view, and return the maximum value.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(db_age) AS db_age
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: DB Age

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the longest transaction duration within the current time window, where the transaction is still active (not yet committed or rolled back). This helps assess long-running transactions, which can lead to increased database age, WAL log bloat, and inability to vacuum outdated tuples.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_activity view.
- Calculate the duration since the earliest active transaction started, or the duration since the earliest prepared transaction was prepared. The resulting time interval is referred to as swell_time.
- The earliest active transaction refers to the one that has actually started a transaction session. A prepared transaction refers to a transaction in the two-phase commit (2PC) protocol — that is, a transaction that has been prepared for commit but has not yet been finalized (committed or rolled back).
- Collection sql：WITH a AS (SELECT MIN(xact_start) m FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE backend_type='client backend' AND (backend_xid IS NOT NULL OR backend_xmin IS NOT NULL)), b AS (SELECT MIN(prepared) m FROM pg_prepared_xacts) SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM NOW()-LEAST(a.m,b.m))::BIGINT FROM a,b

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(swell_time) AS longest_transaction_duration
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Longest Transaction Duration

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the duration of the slowest ongoing SQL query within the current time window to help evaluate SQL execution performance in the database.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- The metric is derived from the pg_stat_activity view, filtered with the conditions `backend_type = 'client backend'` and `state = 'active'`.
- Return the record with the maximum value of `now() - query_start`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(longest_sql_running_time) AS longest_sql_running_time
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Longest SQL Duration

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the replication lag (including send, write, flush, and replay delays) in MB between the primary database and each standby instance or connection that has established streaming replication, within the current time window. The name of each streaming replication connection is also included. This metric is collected only from the read-write (RW) node.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_replication view.
- Data is collected by grouping records in pg_stat_replication by application_name, and calculating the difference between sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, and replay_lsn for each replication stream and the current_lsn on the read-write (RW) node. These differences represent send lag, write lag, flush lag, and replay lag, respectively.
- sent_lsn: The last WAL log position (LSN) sent from the read-write (RW) node to the replication receiver. write_lsn: The last WAL position confirmed to have been written to disk by the replication receiver. flush_lsn: The last WAL position confirmed to have been flushed (fsync) by the replication receiver. In PolarDB, which uses direct I/O, all WAL logs up to write_lsn are effectively already flushed. replay_lsn: The last WAL position confirmed to have been replayed by the replication receiver.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 application_name AS metric,
 AVG(sent_latency_in_bytes) AS sent,
 AVG(write_latency_in_bytes) AS write,
 AVG(flush_latency_in_bytes) AS flush,
 AVG(replay_latency_in_bytes) AS replay
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_pg_stat_replication, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND application_name NOT LIKE 'standby%'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), application_name
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Replication Lag(bytes)

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the replication lag (including write lag, flush lag, and replay lag) in milliseconds between the primary database and each instance or connection that has established streaming replication, within the current time window. The name of each streaming replication connection is also included. This metric is collected only from the read-write (RW) node.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_replication view.
- Data is collected by grouping entries in the pg_stat_replication view by application_name, and retrieving the write_lag, flush_lag, and replay_lag fields as write lag, flush lag, and replay lag, respectively.
- write_lag: The delay between the time a WAL log is received by the replication receiver and the time it is written to the operating system. flush_lag: The delay between the time a WAL log is received by the replication receiver and the time it is flushed (fsync) to disk. replay_lag: The delay between the time a WAL log is received by the replication receiver and the time it is successfully replayed.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 application_name AS metric,
 AVG(replay_lag_in_ms) AS replay,
 AVG(write_lag_in_ms) AS write,
 AVG(flush_lag_in_ms) AS flush
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_pg_stat_replication, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND application_name NOT LIKE 'standby%'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER), application_name
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Replication Lag(time)

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the number of block reads from cache and disk for the database instance within the current time window, which can reflect the block read status and cache hit ratio to a certain extent.
- blks_read：The number of blocks read from disk within the current time window.
- blks_hit：The number of blocks that were found in the buffer cache (i.e., cache hits) within the current time window, avoiding disk I/O.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- The data is obtained from the pg_stat_database view. Since the blks_read and blks_hit fields in this view are cumulative, the difference between their values over the specified time window is calculated to obtain the data for the current period.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(blks_read_delta) AS blks_read,
 AVG(blks_hit_delta) AS blks_hit
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: DB Read

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the number of dirty page writes from buffer to disk by each process (bg_writer, backend, checkpoint) within the current time window. In PolarDB, the dirty page writes from backend processes include all activity excluding bgwriter and checkpoint processes, such as parallel writes and IOWorker operations. This metric reflects the distribution of dirty page writes across different processes in the database instance.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- The data comes from the pg_stat_bgwriter view, a PostgreSQL system view that provides statistics on the background writer process.
- checkpoint: Corresponds to the buffers_checkpoint column in the view, representing the number of buffer blocks written to disk during checkpoint operations.
- bgwriter: Corresponds to the buffers_clean column in the view, representing the number of dirty pages cleaned by the background writer process during its normal operation.
- backend: Corresponds to the buffers_backend column in the view, representing the number of buffer blocks written to disk by other processes.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(buffers_checkpoint_delta) AS checkpoint,
 AVG(buffers_backend_delta) AS backend,
 AVG(buffers_clean_delta) AS bgwriter
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: DB Write

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Terminology Explanation: WP (Write LSN): Represents the current WAL write position on the primary node. It can be queried using `select pg_current_wal_insert_lsn();`. CP (Consistent LSN): Indicates the consistency point, meaning all data up to this LSN has been written to disk. It can be queried using `select polar_consistent_lsn();`. AP (Apply LSN): Represents the replay position on the read-only (RO) nodes. In monitoring, it refers to the minimum replay LSN among all RO nodes. It can be queried using `select polar_oldest_apply_lsn();`. 
- wp_cp_latency_mb: The gap between the latest LSN and the Consistent LSN. A large value may indicate high RW write pressure or a recent checkpoint failure. You can combine the current pressure status of RW with the command `grep -nr 'Checkpoint approved' *error.log` to search for relevant records of ’Checkpoint approved‘ in the error logs. This will help determine whether the Checkpoint was executed properly within the time period, thus aiding in the root cause analysis.
- ap_cp_latency_mb: The gap between the consistency checkpoint position and the RO replay position.
- wp_ap_latency_mb: The gap between the current latest LSN and the RO replay position. A large value indicates significant RO replay delay; you can investigate whether there are inactive replication slots present.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- wp_cp_latency_mb: pg_current_wal_insert_lsn(), polar_consistent_lsn())。
- ap_cp_latency_mb: pg_wal_lsn_diff(polar_oldest_apply_lsn(), polar_consistent_lsn())。
- wp_ap_latency_mb: pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), polar_oldest_apply_lsn())。

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(wp_cp_latency_mb) AS wp_cp_latency_mb,
 AVG(ap_cp_latency_mb) AS ap_cp_latency_mb,
 AVG(wp_ap_latency_mb) AS wp_ap_latency_mb
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: PolarDB LSN Positions

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Relevant terminology explanation: The Checkpoint position refers to the Checkpoint.redo point. In the native logic checkpoint mechanism, Checkpoint.redo is set to the current Write LSN, requiring all data up to that point to be flushed to disk. When PolarDB's lazy checkpoint feature is enabled, Checkpoint.redo is set to the current consistency position, allowing the checkpoint to complete quickly and non-blockingly.
- When the checkpoint position experiences significant latency, it directly affects the amount of log that needs to be replayed during cluster crash recovery. The larger the volume of logs to be replayed, the longer the recovery time, and the greater the potential impact on cluster availability.

The following scenarios may lead to checkpoint position latency:
- Consistency position latency can lead to checkpoint position latency. The cause of consistency position latency should be prioritized for investigation.
- Any factors that cause consistency position latency can also lead to checkpoint position latency. For example, bulk data modifications in the workload or delayed dirty page flushing may contribute to such delays.
- The checkpoint execution cycle is long, and a significant amount of data modifications occur within each cycle.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- select redo_lsn from pg_control_checkpoint();。

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(checkpoint_latency_mb) AS checkpoint延迟
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Checkpoint Delay

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- The metric displays the number of dirty pages in the current time window, reflecting the current update and write pressure on the database instance.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the polar_flushlist() function.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(polar_dirtypage_size) AS polar_dirtypage_size
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: dirty page

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Inactive replication slots should be closely monitored when their count is greater than zero, as they may lead to the risk of WAL log accumulation.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- `select count(*) AS inactive_slots from pg_replication_slots where restart_lsn is not null and active = 'f';`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER) AS time,
 AVG(inactive_slots) AS inactive_slots
FROM polar_gawr_collection.get_data_from_proper_table(NULL::${LogicInstance}.view_fact_dbmetrics, $__unixEpochFrom(), $__unixEpochTo(), ($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
WHERE
 physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
GROUP BY time - time % (($__interval_ms / 1000)::INTEGER)
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Inactive Slots

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays SQL statements that have been executing for more than 5 seconds and are still in progress within the current time window. The information shown includes the process PID, the database name of the query, the type and count of wait events, execution time, and the specific SQL text.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collected based on views such as polar_stat_process.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 pid AS pid, 
 dbname AS dbname, 
 string_agg(wait_event || ':' || wait_event_count, ', ') AS wait_event_and_event_count, 
 MAX(executing_time) / 60 AS max_sample_execution_time_min, 
 query AS query 
FROM (
 SELECT
 pid AS pid,
 dbname AS dbname,
 CASE WHEN wait_event_type = '' THEN 'CPU:CPU' ELSE wait_event_type || ':' || wait_event END AS wait_event,
 query AS query,
 COUNT(*) AS wait_event_count,
 MAX(executing_time) AS executing_time
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_slow_query
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() 
 AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY pid, dbname, CASE WHEN wait_event_type = '' THEN 'CPU:CPU' ELSE wait_event_type || ':' || wait_event END, query
) a 
GROUP BY pid, dbname, query 
ORDER BY 3 DESC;

panels.title: Top Slow Queries

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays the lock contention situation within the current time window. It lists the database, table, lock type, lock level, and the total number of other sessions that were blocked during the period this lock was held.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 datname AS database,
 relation AS relation,
 locktype AS lock_type,
 mode AS lock_mode,
 query AS query,
 SUM(total_num)::BIGINT AS total_blocked_sessions
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_lock_contention_history
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY 数据库, 表, 锁类型, 锁级别, query
 ORDER BY 累计阻塞采样会话数 DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Lock Contentions

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays the top 10 longest-running transactions (>5s) within the current time window, along with detailed information such as application, client, database, user, process, query, status, and wait events.

targets.rawSql:
WITH x AS (
SELECT
 time,
 application_name,
 client,
 dbname,
 username,
 pid,
 query,
 state,
 CASE WHEN wait_event_type='' THEN 'CPU:CPU' ELSE wait_event_type || ':' || wait_event END AS wait_event_union,
 transaction_time AS transaction_time
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_long_transaction
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 ) 
SELECT 
 TO_TIMESTAMP(time)::TEXT AS "last_sample_time",
 x.application_name AS "application_name",
 x.client AS "client",
 x.dbname AS "dbname",
 x.username AS "username",
 x.pid AS "pid",
 x.query AS "last_query",
 x.state AS "state",
 x.wait_event_union AS "wait_event",
 x.transaction_time AS "transaction_time"
 FROM x
 
 JOIN (
SELECT
 application_name,
 client,
 dbname,
 username,
 pid,
 query,
 state,
 wait_event_union,
 MAX(transaction_time) AS max_transaction_time
 FROM x
 
 GROUP BY application_name, client, dbname, username, pid, query, state, wait_event_union
 ORDER BY max_transaction_time DESC LIMIT 10
 ) b ON x.application_name=b.application_name AND x.client=b.client AND x.dbname=b.dbname AND x.username=b.username AND x.pid=b.pid AND x.transaction_time=b.max_transaction_time

panels.title: Top Long-Running Transactions (>5s)
The following data requires the pg_stat_statements extension to be enabled.

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays detailed information about SQL statements with the longest total execution time within the current time window. This includes total execution time, average execution time, IO ratio, physical and logical read/write operations, number of rows processed, and more.
- Physical & Logical Explanation: Logical Read indicates the number of times a page read operation is invoked, including both reading from the buffer (cache hit) and reading from disk (cache miss). Physical read refers to the number of times data is read directly from disk. Logical write and physical write are similar in concept to logical and physical read, respectively. The ratio between physical I/O and logical I/O reflects the cache hit rate during the execution of the SQL statement to some extent.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Obtained by processing the pg_stat_statements, pg_user, and pg_database views.

targets.rawSql:
WITH x AS (
 SELECT * FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_top_sql
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 )
 SELECT queryid AS queryid, query AS query,
 
 ROUND(SUM(total_time)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "total_time(ms)",
 SUM(calls)::BIGINT AS total_calls,
 ROUND((SUM(total_time) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS "avg_time(ms)",
 ROUND((SUM(blk_iotime) / SUM(total_time) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "IO_time(%)",
 SUM(logical_read)::BIGINT AS logical_read,
 SUM(blks_read)::BIGINT AS blks_read,
 SUM(logical_write)::BIGINT AS logical_write,
 SUM(blks_written)::BIGINT AS blks_write,
 SUM(rows)::BIGINT AS rows
 FROM x
 GROUP BY queryid, query
 ORDER BY SUM(total_time) DESC LIMIT 40

panels.title: Top Total Execution Time

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays detailed information about SQL statements with the longest average execution time within the current time window. This includes total execution time, average execution time, IO ratio, physical and logical read/write operations, number of rows processed, and more.
- Physical & Logical Explanation: Logical read indicates the number of times a page read operation is invoked, including both reads from the buffer (cache hit) and reads from disk (cache miss). Physical read refers to the number of times data is read directly from disk. Logical write and physical write are similar to the former. The ratio between physical I/O and logical I/O provides an indication of the cache hit rate during the execution of the SQL statement.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Obtained by processing the pg_stat_statements, pg_user, and pg_database system views.

targets.rawSql:
WITH x AS (
 SELECT * FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_top_sql
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 )
 SELECT queryid, query,
 ROUND(SUM(total_time)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "total_time(ms)",
 SUM(calls)::BIGINT AS calls,
 ROUND((SUM(total_time) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS "avg_time(ms)",
 ROUND((SUM(blk_iotime) / SUM(total_time) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "IO_time(%)",
 SUM(logical_read)::BIGINT AS logical_read,
 SUM(blks_read)::BIGINT AS blks_read,
 SUM(logical_write)::BIGINT AS logical_write,
 SUM(blks_written)::BIGINT AS blks_write,
 SUM(rows)::BIGINT AS rows
 FROM x
 GROUP BY queryid, query
 ORDER BY "avg_time(ms)" DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Average Execution Time

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays detailed information about SQL statements with the longest total read I/O time within the current time window. This includes total read I/O time, average read I/O time, read cache hit ratio, and more.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Obtained by processing the pg_stat_statements, pg_user, pg_database views.

targets.rawSql:
WITH x AS (
 SELECT * FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_top_sql
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 )
 SELECT queryid, query,
 ROUND(SUM(blk_read_time)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "blk_read_time(ms)",
 SUM(calls)::BIGINT AS calls,
 ROUND((SUM(total_time) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS "avg_time(ms)",
 ROUND((SUM(blk_read_time) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS "avg_blk_read_time(ms)",
 ROUND((SUM(blk_read_time) / SUM(total_time) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "blk_read_time(%)",
 ROUND((SUM(logical_read) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_logical_read,
 ROUND((SUM(blks_read) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_blks_read,
 CASE WHEN SUM(logical_read) < 1 THEN 100 ELSE ROUND((SUM(blks_hit) / SUM(logical_read) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) END AS "read_cache_hit(%)",
 ROUND((SUM(rows) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_rows
 FROM x
 GROUP BY queryid, query
 ORDER BY SUM(blk_read_time) DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Read I/O

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays detailed information about SQL statements with the longest total write I/O time within the current time window. This includes total write I/O time, average write I/O time, write cache hit ratio, and more.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Obtained by processing the pg_stat_statements, pg_user, and pg_database views.

targets.rawSql:
WITH x AS (
 SELECT * FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_top_sql
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 )
 SELECT queryid, query,
 ROUND(SUM(blk_write_time)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "total_blk_write_time(ms)",
 SUM(calls)::BIGINT AS calls,
 ROUND((SUM(total_time) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS "avg_calls(ms)",
 ROUND((SUM(blk_write_time) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS "avg_blk_write_time(ms)",
 ROUND((SUM(blk_write_time) / SUM(total_time) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "blk_write_time(%)",
 ROUND((SUM(logical_write) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_logical_write,
 ROUND((SUM(blks_written) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_blks_write,
 CASE WHEN SUM(logical_write) < 1 THEN 100 ELSE ROUND((SUM(blks_hit) / SUM(logical_write) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) END AS "write_cache_hit(%)",
 ROUND((SUM(rows) / SUM(calls))::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_rows
 FROM x
 GROUP BY queryid, query
 ORDER BY SUM(blk_write_time) DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Write I/O
The following data is recommended to be queried with an interval of at least one hour.

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays tables that have undergone a high number of sequential scans within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect data from the pg_stat_user_tables view and filter based on the seq_tup_read column.

targets.rawSql:
WITH x AS (
 SELECT *
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 )
 SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 SUM(seq_scan)::BIGINT AS seq_scan,
 SUM(seq_tup_read)::BIGINT AS seq_tup_read
 FROM x
 GROUP BY dbname, schemaname, relname HAVING SUM(seq_tup_read) > 0.1
 ORDER BY seq_tup_read DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Table-Level Sequential Scans

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays tables that have undergone a high number of index scans within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect data from the pg_stat_user_tables view and filter based on the idx_tup_fetch column.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 SUM(idx_scan)::BIGINT AS idx_scan,
 SUM(idx_tup_fetch)::BIGINT AS idx_tup_fetch
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY dbname, schemaname, relname HAVING SUM(idx_tup_fetch) > 0.1
 ORDER BY idx_tup_fetch DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Table-Level Index Scans

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays tables with the highest total number of modified rows within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect data from the pg_stat_user_tables view and filter based on `n_tup_ins + n_tup_upd + n_tup_del + n_tup_hot_upd`.
- n_tup_ins: The number of tuples inserted into the table.
- n_tup_upd: The number of tuples updated in the table.
- n_tup_hot_upd: The number of tuples updated using HOT.
- n_tup_del: The number of tuples deleted from the table.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 SUM(n_tup_ins)::BIGINT AS insert_rows,
 SUM(n_tup_upd)::BIGINT AS update_rows,
 SUM(n_tup_hot_upd)::BIGINT AS HOT_update_rows,
 SUM(n_tup_del)::BIGINT AS delete_rows,
 SUM(n_tup_modified)::BIGINT AS total_modified_rows
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY dbname, schemaname, relname HAVING SUM(n_tup_modified) > 0.1
 ORDER BY total_modified_rows DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Table-Level Row Changes

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays indexes that have undergone a high number of index scans within the current time window.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect data from the pg_stat_user_indexes view and filter based on the idx_tup_read column.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 indexrelname AS index,
 SUM(idx_scan)::BIGINT AS idx_scan,
 SUM(idx_tup_fetch)::BIGINT AS idx_tup_fetch,
 SUM(idx_tup_read)::BIGINT AS idx_tup_read
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_indexes
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY dbname, schema, relname, index HAVING SUM(idx_tup_read) > 0.1
 ORDER BY idx_scan DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Index Scans

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays tables with a high number of logical reads within the current time window. Logical reads: `heap_blks_read+heap_blks_hit`.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect data from the pg_statio_user_tables view and filter based on the `heap_blks_read+heap_blks_hit`.
- heap_blks_read: The number of table data pages (heap blocks) read from disk.
- heap_blks_hit: The number of table data pages found in cache, avoiding disk reads.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 SUM(heap_logical_read)::BIGINT AS logical_read,
 SUM(heap_blks_read)::BIGINT AS heap_blks_read,
 ROUND((SUM(heap_blks_hit) / SUM(heap_logical_read) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "cache_hit(%)"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_statio_user_tables
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY dbname, schema, relname HAVING SUM(heap_logical_read) > 0.1
 ORDER BY logical_read DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Table-Level Read I/O

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Displays tables with a high number of logical reads via index usage within the current time window. Logical reads: idx_blks_read + idx_blks_hit.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect data from the pg_statio_user_tables view and filter based on `idx_blks_read + idx_blks_hit`.
- heap_blks_read: The number of table data pages (heap blocks) read from disk.
- heap_blks_hit: The number of table data pages found in the cache, thus avoiding a read from disk.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 SUM(idx_logical_read)::BIGINT AS logical_read,
 SUM(idx_blks_read)::BIGINT AS blks_read,
 ROUND((SUM(idx_blks_hit) / (SUM(idx_logical_read)+1) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "cache_hit(%)"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_statio_user_tables
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY dbname, schema, relname HAVING SUM(heap_logical_read) > 0.1
 ORDER BY logical_read DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Table-Level Index Read I/O

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the indexes with the highest number of logical reads on index pages within the current time window. Logical Reads: idx_blks_read+idx_blks_hit。

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Collect the data from the pg_statio_user_tables view, filtering by the sum of idx_blks_read+idx_blks_hitt.
- idx_blks_read: The number of index pages read from disk.
- idx_blks_hit: The number of index pages found in the cache.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 indexrelname AS index,
 SUM(idx_logical_read)::BIGINT AS logical_read,
 SUM(idx_blks_read)::BIGINT AS blks_read,
 ROUND((SUM(idx_blks_hit) / SUM(idx_logical_read) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "cache_hit(%)"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_statio_user_indexes
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
 GROUP BY dbname, schema, relname, index HAVING SUM(idx_logical_read) > 0.1
 ORDER BY logical_read DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Index-Level Read I/O

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display the ordinary tables and TOAST tables with the highest space consumption in the current database instance.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Calculate table size using the pg_class view with the formula: relpages * current_setting('block_size')::BIGINT / 1024 / 1024

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 CASE WHEN relkind='r' THEN 'relname'
 WHEN relkind='i' THEN 'index'
 WHEN relkind='S' THEN 'seq'
 WHEN relkind='t' THEN 'TOAST_rel'
 ELSE 'other' END AS type,
 ROUND(relsize_total::NUMERIC / 1024, 2) AS "space_usage(GB)"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_relation_size
 WHERE time = (SELECT MAX(time) FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_relation_size WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}')
 AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND relsize_total > 0.01
 ORDER BY relsize_total DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Space Consumption

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Assess tables where age consistently exceeds $1.2$ billion, as this hits the autovacuum freeze threshold. This condition suggests autovacuum is failing to complete operations on the table. Execute a manual VACUUM FREEZE command to lower the table's age.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- Calculate table age using the pg_class view by applying the function AGE(relfrozenxid)。

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 CASE WHEN relkind='r' THEN 'relname'
 WHEN relkind='i' THEN 'index'
 WHEN relkind='S' THEN 'seq'
 WHEN relkind='t' THEN 'TOAST_rel'
 ELSE 'other' END AS type,
 table_age::BIGINT AS value_age,
 ROUND((table_age / 2147483647 * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS "wraparound_ratio(%)"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_table_age
 WHERE time = (SELECT MAX(time) FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_table_age WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}')
 ORDER BY table_age DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Table Age

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display tables with a high proportion of dead tuples, as this suggests delayed or insufficient VACUUM processes. Analyze the last VACUUM and AUTOVACUUM times for supplementary diagnosis. Perform a manual VACUUM operation if necessary.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_user_tables view, which provides statistics to determine the necessity of index optimization, VACUUM, or ANALYZE maintenance tasks.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 n_live_tup::BIGINT AS live_tup,
 n_dead_tup::BIGINT AS dead_tup,
 ROUND(dead_tup_ratio::NUMERIC, 2) AS dead_tup_ratio_percent,
 CASE WHEN last_vacuum < 0.1 THEN 'Not performed'
 ELSE ROUND(((time - last_vacuum) / 60)::NUMERIC, 1)::TEXT END AS since_last_vacuum_min,
 CASE WHEN last_autovacuum < 0.1 THEN 'Not performed'
 ELSE ROUND(((time - last_autovacuum) / 60)::NUMERIC, 1)::TEXT END AS since_last_autovacuum_min
FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables
WHERE time = (
 SELECT MAX(time)
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo()
 AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
)
AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}'
ORDER BY dead_tup_ratio DESC
LIMIT 10;

panels.title: Top Bloat Ratio

panels.description:
Metric Description: 
- Display tables with a high number of updated rows following ANALYZE execution. Note that excessive row updates after ANALYZE can result in stale statistics, potentially impairing the selection of the optimal SQL execution plan.

Metric Source & Collection Method: 
- From the pg_stat_user_tables view, which provides statistics to determine the necessity of index optimization, VACUUM, or ANALYZE maintenance tasks.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
 dbname AS dbname,
 schemaname AS schema,
 relname AS relname,
 n_mod_since_analyze::BIGINT AS since_analyze_update_rows,
 CASE WHEN last_analyze<0.1 THEN 'Not performed' ELSE ROUND(((time - last_analyze) / 60)::NUMERIC, 1)::TEXT END AS "since_last_analyze(min)",
 CASE WHEN last_autoanalyze<0.1 THEN 'Not performed' ELSE ROUND(((time - last_autoanalyze) / 60)::NUMERIC, 1)::TEXT END AS "since_last_autoanalyze(min)"
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables
 WHERE time = (SELECT MAX(time) FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_stat_user_tables WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}')
 ORDER BY n_mod_since_analyze DESC LIMIT 10

panels.title: Top Analyze Row Update Counts

targets.rawSql:
SELECT name AS name, source AS source, MAX(setting) AS value, (TO_TIMESTAMP(MAX(time)))::TEXT AS sample_time
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_settings
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND source NOT IN ('client', 'session')
 GROUP BY name, source
 ORDER BY
 CASE source
 WHEN 'configuration file' THEN 1
 WHEN 'override' THEN 2
 WHEN 'default' THEN 3
 ELSE 4 END

SELECT name AS name, source AS source, MAX(setting) AS value1, MIN(setting) AS value2 
 FROM ${LogicInstance}.view_fact_polar_settings
 WHERE time BETWEEN $__unixEpochFrom() AND $__unixEpochTo() AND physical_ins_name='${PhysicalInstance}' AND source NOT IN ('client', 'session')
 GROUP BY name, source
 HAVING MAX(setting) != MIN(setting)
 ORDER BY
 CASE source
 WHEN 'configuration file' THEN 1
 WHEN 'override' THEN 2
 WHEN 'default' THEN 3
 ELSE 4 END
